name: python3-gpg
title: python3-gpg
summary: Python interface to the GPGME GnuPG encryption library (Python 3)
description:
Python interface to the GPGME GnuPG encryption library (Python 3)
The "gpg" Python module is, for the most part, a direct interface to
the C GPGME library.  However, it is re-packaged in a more Pythonic
way -- object-oriented with classes and modules.  Take a look at the
classes defined here -- they correspond directly to certain object
types in GPGME for C.
.
Features:
* Feature-rich, full implementation of the GPGME library. Supports
all GPGME features except interactive editing (coming soon).
Callback functions may be written in pure Python.
* Ability to sign, encrypt, decrypt, and verify data.
* Ability to list keys, export and import keys, and manage the keyring.
* Fully object-oriented with convenient classes and modules.
.
This is the official upstream Python 3 binding for GPGME.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use python3-gpg because Python interface to the GPGME GnuPG encryption library (Python 3).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use python3-gpg as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair python3-gpg with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `python3-gpg --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
